summary: Iteration 3 (Archive/AI expansion) tested end-to-end. Backend: 14/14 pytest passed (register, bookmarks CRUD, rate-limit 429 after 5 fails, import-extras idempotent, tools count>=124, admin login regression). Frontend: all 9 flows verified via Playwright on public URL — Directory shows 124 tools, Compare page renders 3-col table with picker/remove, register->auto-login->/bookmarks, bookmark toggle (Save->Saved) on ToolDetail, unauth bookmark shows sign-in toast, nav swaps Sign In <-> Saved+Logout, admin login still works, Import Extras button visible on /admin/tools.

integration_issues.issue: AuthPage.submit always calls nav('/bookmarks') on success; a super_admin logging in via /login lands on /bookmarks instead of /admin. Layout still allows access to /admin manually. Minor UX regression — admins previously were routed to /admin.

verified_behaviors: GET /api/tools returns 124 tools; directory UI shows '124 tools found' (PASS)
POST /api/auth/register creates role='user', returns token+user (no password_hash), sets httpOnly cookie; duplicate email -> 400; password<6 -> 400
GET /api/auth/me returns new user with Bearer token
POST /api/bookmarks/chatgpt (auth'd) -> ok:true; GET /api/bookmarks lists it; DELETE removes it; POST for invalid slug -> 404; all bookmark endpoints require auth (401)
5 wrong logins -> 401 each; 6th -> 429; even correct password 429 while locked (keyed by email)
POST /api/admin/import-extras: requires admin auth; returns {added, total_available>=90}; second call added=0 (idempotent)
Admin regression login (soumyaranjansrb9@gmail.com / Srb123@#) still returns super_admin token
UI: /register form submits and redirects to /bookmarks; nav shows nav-bookmarks + nav-logout when auth'd, nav-signin when guest
UI: /tools/chatgpt bookmark-btn toggles 'Save' <-> 'Saved'; unauth click shows sign-in toast
UI: /compare?tools=chatgpt,claude,cursor renders 3 columns with compare-remove-<slug> for each; compare-add-btn opens picker modal; compare-pick-<slug> adds tool; remove works
action_items: (Minor UX) In /app/frontend/src/pages/AuthPage.jsx submit(), route admins to /admin: after login, if user.role in ('admin','super_admin') -> nav('/admin'), else nav('/bookmarks'). Currently everyone lands on /bookmarks.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: register() sets httponly cookie but returns token; that's fine, but doc.pop('_id', None) happens AFTER insert — insert_one mutates doc with ObjectId; the pop is present and correct. OK.
server.py: bulk_import passes {'pricing':'freemium','url':''} defaults before **raw — this silently sets pricing='freemium' for any tool missing pricing; consider validating instead of defaulting.

server.py auto-runs import-extras on startup; if the import job partially runs then a Tool(**t) throws, exception is caught with logger.exception but insert_many is atomic — no partial state expected, ok.
AuthPage always nav('/bookmarks') regardless of role — see action item.
server.py is ~800 lines and mixes auth, tools, bookmarks, categories, packs, admin, chat, analytics — recommend splitting into routers for maintainability (>700 line guideline).
test_credentials: Super Admin: soumyaranjansrb9@gmail.com / Srb123@#. Test user created per-run (qa_ui_<ts>@example.com / testpass1).
seed_data_creation: Created ephemeral users via /api/auth/register during tests; login_attempts records for rate-limit test remain (locked for 15min on random emails, harmless).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 3 features all working. The only minor issue is admin post-login route lands on /bookmarks instead of /admin (client-side nav in AuthPage.submit). Rate-limit test file uses fresh emails so does not affect existing accounts. Backend startup auto-imports extras — safe to rerun.
rca of the issue: Admin redirect: AuthPage.jsx submit() calls nav('/bookmarks') unconditionally after login. The role-check useEffect only handles user role. Fix: branch nav target by role. Not a blocker; admin manually navigates to /admin sidebar link.
